---
title: "Mike Konczal"
aliases:
  - about.html
  - portfolio.html
  - writings.html
  - clips.html
  - freedom_from_the_market.html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
image: images/mkonczal_index.jpg
---

```{=html}
<!-- The page title ("Mike Konczal") is already the sidebar brand, so hide the
     Quarto title-block H1 on this page only. It stays in the document metadata
     for the browser tab and social cards. -->
<style>#title-block-header { display: none; }</style>
```

::: {#top .mk-hero}
<img class="mk-hero__photo" src="images/mkonczal_index.jpg" alt="Mike Konczal">

::: {.mk-hero__bio}
Mike Konczal is the Vice-President of Policy and Research at the [Economic Security Project](https://www.economicsecurityproject.org/), where he oversees policy development, research, and strategic analysis to build economic power for all Americans. Previously, he served as a Special Assistant to the President for Economic Policy and Chief Economist at the National Economic Council.

A former software and financial engineer, he was an early hire at the Roosevelt Institute, where he led major projects on financial reform, inequality, economic ideas, and macroeconomics. He is the author of [*Freedom from the Market*](#book) (New Press, 2021), described by the *Financial Times* as "a powerful polemic," and co-author of [*Rewriting the Rules of the American Economy*](https://wwnorton.com/books/rewriting-the-rules-of-the-american-economy/) (W.W. Norton, 2015) with Joseph Stiglitz. He holds a B.A. in math and computer science and an M.S. in finance from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

Specializing in economic data analysis and policy research, Mike is a respected voice in U.S. economic discourse, frequently cited in media outlets, and has provided testimony before Congress. The *New York Times Magazine* has described him as having "a cult following among progressives." Born and raised in Chicago, he now lives in Takoma Park, Maryland, with his wife, two daughters, and pit bull.
:::
:::

<!-- ==========================================================================
     MACRO STRIP — live headline indicators, fetched from FRED at render time.
     Data policy: fetch fresh from fredgraph.csv (no API key needed); on
     success, cache to data/macro_strip.csv. If the fetch fails, fall back to
     the cache; if there is no cache either, FAIL the render loudly rather
     than ship stale-looking or fabricated numbers.
     ========================================================================== -->

::: {#macro-heading}
Data I'm watching
:::

::: {#macro-strip .mk-macro}

```{r macro_strip, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(htmltools)
source("scripts/build_macro.R")   # defines build_macro(): the nine indicators,
                                  # already transformed, in long (date, series_id,
                                  # value) form. See that file for the economics.

cache_path <- "data/macro_strip.csv"

# Fetch fresh; on any failure (e.g. BLS_KEY missing, network) fall back to the
# cached vintage rather than shipping a broken strip, and only fail the render
# outright if there is no cache at all.
macro <- tryCatch(
  {
    out <- build_macro()
    write_csv(out, cache_path)
    out
  },
  error = function(e) {
    if (file.exists(cache_path)) {
      message("Macro fetch failed; using cached data: ", conditionMessage(e))
      read_csv(cache_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>% mutate(date = as.Date(date))
    } else {
      stop("Macro strip: fetch failed and no cache at ", cache_path,
           " — ", conditionMessage(e))
    }
  }
)

series_of <- function(id) macro %>% filter(series_id == id) %>% arrange(date)
u  <- series_of("UNRATE_FULL")    # unemployment rate, extra digit (percent)
sc <- series_of("SUPERCORE_YOY")  # PCE services ex-energy & housing, y/y percent
vu <- series_of("VU")             # vacancies per unemployed worker (ratio)
di <- series_of("DIFFUSION")      # 1-month payroll diffusion index (0-100)
ws <- series_of("WOMEN_SHARE")    # women's share of trailing-12m net job gains (%)
hs <- series_of("HEALTH_SHARE")   # ed & health share of trailing-12m net gains (%)
ls <- series_of("LABOR_SHARE")    # nonfarm business labor share (index 2017=100)
ty <- series_of("TAYLOR")         # Taylor-rule policy rate estimate (percent)
ff <- series_of("FEDFUNDS")       # effective federal funds rate (percent)

# --- Sanity checks: fail the render rather than publish a nonsense number ---
# The two share-of-gains ratios are volatile (a small denominator swings them),
# so their bounds are deliberately wide — just enough to catch a broken pull.
stopifnot(
  between(tail(u$value, 1),  1.5, 15),
  between(tail(sc$value, 1), -3,  12),
  between(tail(vu$value, 1), 0.1, 5),
  between(tail(di$value, 1), 0,   100),
  between(tail(ws$value, 1), -100, 200),
  between(tail(hs$value, 1), -100, 300),
  between(tail(ls$value, 1), 40,  70),
  between(tail(ty$value, 1), -5,  20),
  between(tail(ff$value, 1), 0,   15)
)

# Sparkline as inline SVG. Each of the last `n` observations gets a transparent
# hit-circle carrying a data-tip attribute (month + value); a small script under
# the strip shows it as a tooltip on hover. fmt formats the value the same way
# as the headline number for that series.
# viewBox scales with the container width set in CSS; width/height here are the
# intrinsic size. pad_x keeps the start/end dots off the edges (otherwise the
# non-uniform scaling clips the end dot's right half); pad_y is small so the
# line uses most of the box height instead of leaving dead space top and bottom.
spark_win_months <- 36  # trailing window drawn (~3 years; works monthly OR quarterly)

# Restrict to the trailing window, over rows where the primary series exists.
# A window in *months* (not a fixed observation count) shows a comparable 3-year
# span whether the series is monthly (~37 points) or quarterly (~13). `v2` — the
# optional second line — is assumed aligned to `dates`/`v` (same length, same
# non-NA rows), which holds for the Taylor-rule vs fed-funds pair (one pull).
spark_slice <- function(dates, v, v2 = NULL) {
  keep <- !is.na(v)
  d <- dates[keep]; v <- v[keep]; v2 <- if (is.null(v2)) NULL else v2[keep]
  sel <- d >= (max(d) %m-% months(spark_win_months - 1))
  list(dates = d[sel], v = v[sel], v2 = if (is.null(v2)) NULL else v2[sel])
}

# `ref`, when set, draws a muted dotted horizontal reference line at that value
# (e.g. 50 for the diffusion index, 1 for v/u): no data-tip and pointer-events
# :none, so it never appears in the hover tracking; its value is an HTML chip
# (not SVG <text>, which the non-uniform scaling would distort) placed at the
# line's height — the viewBox height equals the CSS pixel height, so viewBox y
# maps 1:1 to pixels. `v2` overlays a second line (shared y-scale) for the
# Taylor-rule/fed-funds chart; `lab`/`lab2` prefix each line's hover tips.
spark_svg <- function(dates, v, fmt, ref = NULL, ref_fmt = NULL, v2 = NULL,
                      col = "#7fb3d8", col2 = "#ef9a9a", dot = "#ffffff",
                      lab = NULL, lab2 = NULL, w = 150, h = 100) {
  s <- spark_slice(dates, v, v2); dates <- s$dates; v <- s$v; v2 <- s$v2
  # Shared vertical domain across both lines and the reference value.
  rng <- range(c(v, v2, ref), na.rm = TRUE); if (diff(rng) == 0) rng <- rng + c(-1, 1)
  pad_x <- 5; pad_y <- 5
  scale_y <- function(val) h - pad_y - (val - rng[1]) / diff(rng) * (h - 2 * pad_y)
  x <- seq(pad_x, w - pad_x, length.out = length(v))

  # Building blocks reused by each line.
  poly   <- function(yv, color) sprintf(
    '<polyline points="%s" fill="none" stroke="%s" stroke-width="2.2" stroke-linejoin="round"/>',
    paste(sprintf("%.1f,%.1f", x, yv), collapse = " "), color)
  enddot <- function(yv, color) sprintf(
    '<circle cx="%.1f" cy="%.1f" r="3.2" fill="%s"/>', tail(x, 1), tail(yv, 1), color)
  hits   <- function(yv, vv, pref) paste(
    mapply(function(xi, yi, di, vi)
      sprintf(paste0('<circle class="mk-macro__pt" cx="%.1f" cy="%.1f" r="5" ',
                     'fill="transparent" data-tip="%s%s: %s"></circle>'),
              xi, yi, pref, format(di, "%b %Y"), fmt(vi)),
      x, yv, dates, vv), collapse = "")
  # HTML entity (not a literal middot) so the separator survives R's sprintf
  # regardless of locale; the browser decodes &#183; back to "·" in the tip.
  pref <- function(l) if (is.null(l)) "" else paste0(l, " &#183; ")

  # Reference line (drawn first, under the data) + its HTML label.
  ref_line <- ""; ref_label <- ""
  if (!is.null(ref)) {
    yr <- scale_y(ref)
    ref_line <- sprintf(
      paste0('<line class="mk-macro__ref" x1="0" y1="%.1f" x2="%d" y2="%.1f" ',
             'vector-effect="non-scaling-stroke" pointer-events="none"></line>'), yr, w, yr)
    ref_txt <- if (is.null(ref_fmt)) formatC(ref, format = "g") else ref_fmt(ref)
    ref_label <- sprintf('<span class="mk-macro__ref-label" style="top:%.1fpx">%s</span>',
                         yr, ref_txt)
  }

  # Second line, if present, drawn beneath the primary.
  line2 <- ""
  if (!is.null(v2)) {
    y2 <- scale_y(v2)
    line2 <- paste0(poly(y2, col2), enddot(y2, col2), hits(y2, v2, pref(lab2)))
  }
  y <- scale_y(v)

  svg <- sprintf(
    paste0('<svg class="mk-macro__spark" viewBox="0 0 %d %d" width="%d" height="%d" role="img" preserveAspectRatio="none">',
           '%s%s%s%s%s</svg>'),
    w, h, w, h, ref_line, line2, poly(y, col), enddot(y, dot), hits(y, v, pref(lab)))
  sprintf('<div class="mk-macro__spark-wrap">%s%s</div>', svg, ref_label)
}

# First/last date of the window actually drawn, so the date row labels the true
# span, ending at the latest observation.
spark_window <- function(dates, v) {
  d <- spark_slice(dates, v)$dates
  list(start = min(d), end = max(d))
}

pct1 <- function(x) sprintf("%.1f%%", x)
pct2 <- function(x) sprintf("%.2f%%", x)
# Two significant figures for the v/u ratio: one decimal at/above 1 (1.0),
# two below (0.95), none in double digits — never a misleading trailing zero.
sig2 <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "f",
                            digits = max(0, 1 - floor(log10(abs(x)))))
idx1 <- function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)
pct0 <- function(x) sprintf("%.0f%%", x)
or_default <- function(x, d) if (is.null(x)) d else x

fed_col <- "#7fb3d8"  # effective fed funds (primary line, matches the strip blue)
tay_col <- "#ef9a9a"  # Taylor rule (muted coral, legible on the dark band)

cells <- list(
  list(label = "Unemployment",         value = pct2(tail(u$value, 1)),  dates = u$date,  series = u$value,  fmt = pct2, d = max(u$date),  ref = NULL),
  list(label = "Supercore PCE (y/y)",   value = pct1(tail(sc$value, 1)), dates = sc$date, series = sc$value, fmt = pct1, d = max(sc$date), ref = NULL),
  # v/u = 1 (job openings equal to unemployed) and diffusion = 50 (as many
  # industries adding jobs as cutting) are the meaningful balance points.
  list(label = "V/U ratio",             value = sig2(tail(vu$value, 1)), dates = vu$date, series = vu$value, fmt = sig2, d = max(vu$date), ref = 1),
  list(label = "Jobs diffusion index",  value = idx1(tail(di$value, 1)), dates = di$date, series = di$value, fmt = idx1, d = max(di$date), ref = 50),
  # Row 2 -------------------------------------------------------------------
  list(label = "Women's share of job gains (12m)", value = pct0(tail(ws$value, 1)), dates = ws$date, series = ws$value, fmt = pct0, d = max(ws$date), ref = 50, ref_fmt = pct0),
  list(label = "Health care share of job gains (12m)", value = pct0(tail(hs$value, 1)), dates = hs$date, series = hs$value, fmt = pct0, d = max(hs$date), ref = 50, ref_fmt = pct0),
  list(label = "Labor share",                value = pct1(tail(ls$value, 1)), dates = ls$date, series = ls$value, fmt = pct1, d = max(ls$date)),
  # Two lines, shared percent scale: fed funds (primary line) and the Taylor
  # rule. The headline shows both current values, colour-keyed to the lines and
  # ordered Taylor-then-fed to match the title.
  list(label = "Taylor rule vs. fed funds", dual = TRUE,
       value = tagList(
         tags$span(style = paste0("color:", tay_col), pct1(tail(ty$value, 1))),
         tags$span(class = "mk-macro__vs", "vs"),
         tags$span(style = paste0("color:", fed_col), pct1(tail(ff$value, 1)))),
       dates = ff$date, series = ff$value, fmt = pct1, d = max(ff$date),
       v2 = ty$value, col = fed_col, col2 = tay_col, dot = fed_col, lab = "Fed funds", lab2 = "Taylor",
       legend = tags$div(class = "mk-macro__legend",
         tags$span(class = "mk-macro__leg",
                   tags$span(class = "mk-macro__swatch", style = paste0("background:", tay_col)), "Taylor rule"),
         tags$span(class = "mk-macro__leg",
                   tags$span(class = "mk-macro__swatch", style = paste0("background:", fed_col)), "Fed funds")))
)

# The "Data I'm watching" heading now lives outside this band (as a text label
# above it, on the page background) and the render caption has been dropped, so
# the band holds only the graphics. Eight cells flow into the four-column grid
# as two rows.
tags$div(class = "mk-macro__cells",
  !!!map(cells, function(cc) {
    win <- spark_window(cc$dates, cc$series)
    tags$div(class = "mk-macro__cell",
      tags$div(class = "mk-macro__label", cc$label),
      tags$div(class = if (isTRUE(cc$dual)) "mk-macro__value mk-macro__value--dual" else "mk-macro__value",
               cc$value),
      # NB: use [[ ]] (exact), not $, for `lab` — `cc$lab` would partial-match
      # the cell's `label` field and prefix every single-line tooltip with the
      # chart title.
      HTML(spark_svg(cc$dates, cc$series, cc$fmt,
                     ref  = cc[["ref"]], ref_fmt = cc[["ref_fmt"]], v2 = cc[["v2"]],
                     col  = or_default(cc[["col"]],  "#7fb3d8"),
                     col2 = or_default(cc[["col2"]], "#ef9a9a"),
                     dot  = or_default(cc[["dot"]],  "#ffffff"),
                     lab  = cc[["lab"]], lab2 = cc[["lab2"]])),
      cc$legend,  # NULL for single-line cells; htmltools drops NULL children
      # Date row spans the sparkline: window start on the left, latest
      # observation on the right (under the end dot) so it reads left-to-right.
      tags$div(class = "mk-macro__dates",
        tags$span(class = "mk-macro__date mk-macro__date--start", format(win$start, "%b %Y")),
        tags$span(class = "mk-macro__date mk-macro__date--end",   format(win$end,   "%b %Y")))
    )
  })
)
```

:::

```{=html}
<script>
// Hover tooltip for the macro sparklines: each point carries data-tip
// ("[series ·] Mon YYYY: value"). Shown as a small floating box on two lines
// (label/date above value) so it stays narrow; its centre is clamped to the
// viewport so points at the right edge are not clipped.
(function () {
  var strip = document.getElementById("macro-strip");
  if (!strip) return;
  var tip = document.createElement("div");
  tip.className = "mk-macro__tip";
  tip.hidden = true;
  document.body.appendChild(tip);
  // Render "date: value" on two lines: everything before the last ": " on top,
  // the value below. (The value and date never contain ": " themselves.)
  function fill(text) {
    tip.textContent = "";
    var i = text.lastIndexOf(": ");
    if (i < 0) { tip.textContent = text; return; }
    tip.appendChild(document.createTextNode(text.slice(0, i)));
    tip.appendChild(document.createElement("br"));
    var val = document.createElement("span");
    val.className = "mk-macro__tip-val";
    val.textContent = text.slice(i + 2);
    tip.appendChild(val);
  }
  strip.addEventListener("mousemove", function (e) {
    var t = e.target;
    var text = t && t.getAttribute && t.getAttribute("data-tip");
    if (text) {
      fill(text);
      tip.hidden = false;
      var half = tip.offsetWidth / 2, m = 6;
      var x = Math.min(Math.max(e.clientX, half + m), window.innerWidth - half - m);
      tip.style.left = x + "px";
      tip.style.top = e.clientY + "px";
    } else {
      tip.hidden = true;
    }
  });
  strip.addEventListener("mouseleave", function () { tip.hidden = true; });
})();
</script>
```

## Latest {#latest}

A running shortlist of my most recent work.

```{r recent_work, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(htmltools)

fix_title_case <- function(x) {
  x <- str_to_title(x)
  for (w in c(" A "," An "," The "," And "," But "," For "," Of "," In "," On "," To "," With ")) {
    x <- str_replace_all(x, w, str_to_lower(w))
  }
  # strip stray smart-quote bytes that snuck into the CSV
  x <- str_remove_all(x, "[\u00d4\u00d5]")
  x
}

recent <- read_csv("data/work_database.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    Title = fix_title_case(`Original Title`),
    Date  = as.Date(Date),  # ISO YYYY-MM-DD in the CSV
    Type  = str_replace_all(Format, "White Paper", "Research")
  ) %>%
  filter(Highlight == "X") %>%
  arrange(desc(Date)) %>%
  head(5)   # five most recent highlighted items

items <- pmap(recent, function(Date, Title, Outlet, Type, Link, ...) {
  tags$a(
    class = "mk-recent__item",
    href  = Link,
    target = "_blank",
    rel = "noopener",
    tags$div(class = "mk-recent__date",
             format(Date, "%b %d, %Y")),
    tags$div(class = "mk-recent__body",
             tags$div(class = "mk-recent__title", Title),
             tags$div(class = "mk-recent__meta",
                      tags$span(class = "mk-recent__outlet", Outlet))),
    tags$div(class = "mk-recent__type", Type)
  )
})

# Make the whole row a link by switching the wrapper element
htmltools::tagList(
  tags$div(class = "mk-recent", !!!items)
)
```

## Start Here {#start-here}

New here? These are the pieces that best explain what I work on.

```{r start_here, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(htmltools)

fix_title_case <- function(x) {
  x <- str_to_title(x)
  for (w in c(" A "," An "," The "," And "," But "," For "," Of "," In "," On "," To "," With ")) {
    x <- str_replace_all(x, w, str_to_lower(w))
  }
  # ASCII \u escapes, NOT literal smart-quote chars: quarto transliterates
  # non-ASCII source bytes to "<U+00D4>" text, which would turn this class into
  # [<U+00D4>...] and silently strip D/U/5/0/4 from every title.
  str_remove_all(x, "[\\u00d4\\u00d5]")
}

# Curation lives in data/start_here.csv (an ordered list keyed by Link, managed
# from the Site Manager app's "Start here" tab). Title/outlet/date/type and the
# descriptive Excerpt come from the work database so they can't drift; a row may
# still carry its own values (the book, which isn't in the database). The line
# under each title is the item's Excerpt.
blank_na <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  ifelse(is.na(x) | str_trim(x) %in% c("", "NA"), NA_character_, x)
}

db <- read_csv("data/work_database.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  transmute(
    Link,
    DBTitle   = fix_title_case(`Original Title`),
    DBOutlet  = Outlet,
    DBYear    = year(as.Date(Date)),
    DBExcerpt = Excerpt,
    DBType    = case_when(
      str_detect(Outlet, "Testimony") ~ "Testimony",
      Format == "White Paper"         ~ "Research",
      TRUE                            ~ Format
    )
  )

sh <- read_csv("data/start_here.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  left_join(db, by = "Link") %>%
  mutate(
    Title  = coalesce(blank_na(Title),  DBTitle),
    Outlet = coalesce(blank_na(Outlet), DBOutlet),
    Year   = coalesce(Year, DBYear),
    Type   = coalesce(blank_na(Type),   DBType),
    Desc   = coalesce(blank_na(Excerpt), blank_na(DBExcerpt))
  ) %>%
  arrange(Rank)

# Every curated row must resolve to a title; catch broken Links at render time.
stopifnot(!any(is.na(sh$Title)))

items <- pmap(sh, function(Link, Title, Outlet, Year, Desc, ...) {
  internal <- str_starts(Link, "#")
  # No type pill; the outlet and year sit on the right ("Vox, 2015").
  tags$a(
    class  = "mk-start__item",
    href   = Link,
    target = if (!internal) "_blank",
    rel    = if (!internal) "noopener",
    tags$div(class = "mk-start__body",
      tags$div(class = "mk-start__title", Title),
      if (!is.na(Desc)) tags$div(class = "mk-start__impact", HTML(Desc))),
    tags$div(class = "mk-start__meta",
      tags$span(class = "mk-start__outlet", paste0(Outlet, ", ", Year)))
  )
})

tags$div(class = "mk-start", !!!items)
```

## Portfolio {#portfolio}

A combined record of my work: writing, research, media appearances, and press hits. Use the tabs to switch between them, or search within any category. Feel free to contact me if you'd like me to write for you or join you on air.

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(DT)
library(htmltools)

fix_title_case <- function(x) {
  x <- str_to_title(x)
  for (w in c(" A "," An "," The "," And "," But "," For "," Of "," In "," On "," To "," With ")) {
    x <- str_replace_all(x, w, str_to_lower(w))
  }
  # ASCII \u escapes, NOT literal smart-quote chars: quarto transliterates
  # non-ASCII source bytes to "<U+00D4>" text, which would turn this class into
  # [<U+00D4>...] and silently strip D/U/5/0/4 from every title.
  str_remove_all(x, "[\\u00d4\\u00d5]")
}

raw <- read_csv("data/work_database.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    Title = fix_title_case(`Original Title`),
    Date  = as.Date(Date)  # ISO YYYY-MM-DD in the CSV
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Date))

# Map each Format to a top-level tab. To add a tab later, extend this mapping
# and add a matching <button> in the `tabs` chunk below.
cat_for <- function(format) {
  case_when(
    format %in% c("Article", "Book Review")          ~ "Writing",
    format == "White Paper"                          ~ "Research",
    format %in% c("TV", "Radio", "Podcast", "Panel") ~ "Media",
    format == "Quote"                                ~ "Press",
    TRUE                                             ~ NA_character_
  )
}

# Label shown in the Type pill (distinct from the tab/Category above).
type_label <- function(format) {
  case_when(
    format == "White Paper" ~ "Research",
    format == "Quote"       ~ "Press",
    TRUE                    ~ format
  )
}

combined <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    Category = cat_for(Format),
    TypeLab  = type_label(Format)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Category)) %>%
  mutate(
    Year      = year(Date),
    Month     = format(Date, "%b"),
    TitleHTML = paste0(
      '<a href="', Link, '" target="_blank" rel="noopener">', Title, '</a>',
      '<span class="mk-outlet">', Outlet, '</span>'
    ),
    TypeHTML  = paste0('<span class="mk-pill" data-type="', TypeLab, '">', TypeLab, '</span>')
  ) %>%
  select(Category, Year, Month, Type = TypeHTML, Title = TitleHTML)

# Counts per tab, in display order.
tab_n      <- function(cat) sum(combined$Category == cat)
n_writing  <- tab_n("Writing")
n_research <- tab_n("Research")
n_media    <- tab_n("Media")
n_press    <- tab_n("Press")
```

```{r tabs, results='asis'}
cat(sprintf('
<div id="mk-portfolio-tabs" class="mk-tabs" role="tablist" aria-label="Filter portfolio by type">
  <button type="button" class="mk-tab is-active" role="tab" data-cat="Writing" aria-selected="true">Writing<span class="mk-tab__count">%d</span></button>
  <button type="button" class="mk-tab" role="tab" data-cat="Research" aria-selected="false">Research<span class="mk-tab__count">%d</span></button>
  <button type="button" class="mk-tab" role="tab" data-cat="Media" aria-selected="false">Media<span class="mk-tab__count">%d</span></button>
  <button type="button" class="mk-tab" role="tab" data-cat="Press" aria-selected="false">Press<span class="mk-tab__count">%d</span></button>
</div>
', n_writing, n_research, n_media, n_press))
```

::: {.mk-table}

```{r portfolio_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
datatable(
  combined,
  rownames = FALSE,
  escape   = FALSE,
  class    = "row-border compact",
  callback = JS("
    var labels = { Writing: 'Search writing…', Research: 'Search research…', Media: 'Search media…', Press: 'Search press…' };
    var box = $(table.table().container()).find('input[type=search]');
    function activate(cat) {
      $('#mk-portfolio-tabs .mk-tab').each(function() {
        var on = this.getAttribute('data-cat') === cat;
        this.classList.toggle('is-active', on);
        this.setAttribute('aria-selected', on ? 'true' : 'false');
      });
      box.attr('placeholder', labels[cat] || '');
      table.column(0).search(cat).draw();
    }
    $('#mk-portfolio-tabs .mk-tab').on('click', function() {
      activate(this.getAttribute('data-cat'));
    });
    activate('Writing');
  "),
  options  = list(
    pageLength   = 5,
    lengthMenu   = c(5, 10, 25, 50, 100),
    dom          = "<'top'fl>rt<'bottom'ip>",
    autoWidth    = FALSE,
    order        = list(list(1, 'desc')),
    columnDefs   = list(
      list(targets = 0, visible = FALSE, searchable = TRUE),
      list(targets = 1, className = "mk-col-year"),
      list(targets = 2, className = "mk-col-month"),
      list(targets = 3, className = "mk-col-type", orderable = FALSE),
      list(targets = 4, className = "mk-col-title")
    ),
    language = list(
      search       = "",
      searchPlaceholder = "Search writing…",
      lengthMenu   = "Show _MENU_",
      info         = "_START_–_END_ of _TOTAL_",
      infoEmpty    = "No results",
      infoFiltered = "(filtered from _MAX_)",
      paginate     = list(previous = "‹ Prev", `next` = "Next ›")
    )
  )
)
```

:::

You can [check out my resume here](resume.html).

## Projects {#projects}

Open-source tools I build and maintain, for economic data analysis and for writing.

```{=html}
<div class="mk-projects">
  <a class="mk-project" href="tidyusmacro.html">
    <div class="mk-project__media mk-project__media--hex">
      <img src="images/tidyusmacro_hex.png" alt="tidyusmacro hex sticker logo">
    </div>
    <div class="mk-project__body">
      <div class="mk-project__title">tidyusmacro</div>
      <p class="mk-project__desc">An R package that downloads and tidies U.S. macroeconomic data from BLS, BEA, and FRED flat files, returning consistent data frames ready for modeling and graphics seconds after a release goes live.</p>
      <span class="mk-project__cta">Package site &amp; function reference</span>
    </div>
  </a>
  <a class="mk-project" href="draftwatch.html">
    <div class="mk-project__media">
      <img src="images/draftwatch_image.png" alt="Draftwatch app showing a git word-diff of an AI agent's edits">
    </div>
    <div class="mk-project__body">
      <div class="mk-project__title">Draftwatch</div>
      <p class="mk-project__desc">A lightweight IDE for writers to review an AI agent's edits like a pull request: the exact git-backed word diff of what changed, with each change kept or reverted at a click.</p>
      <span class="mk-project__cta">About Draftwatch</span>
    </div>
  </a>
</div>
```

## Freedom from the Market {#book}

::: {.mk-book}
<img class="mk-book__cover" src="images/image-803426826.png" alt="Freedom from the Market book cover">

::: {.mk-book__praise}
### Praise for *Freedom from the Market*

"Konczal is one of the warriors in this fight, arguing fiercely for the need to set much narrower limits on what is left to markets than has been the case in recent decades. A powerful polemic."\
--- Martin Wolf, [*Financial Times*](https://www.ft.com/content/239f31cb-57a3-43d3-ab3d-d18d068f4994)

"By identifying an alternative grammar, one that is grounded in the American past, Freedom from the Market provides a way out of the political cul-de-sac created by the failure of the market to deliver on its promises of 'freedom.'"\
--- Molly Michelmore, [*Democracy: A Journal of Ideas*](https://democracyjournal.org/magazine/61/freedoms-just-another-word/)

"Freedom from the Market is an impressive book, easily one of the best I've read in the past several years. I cannot recommend it highly enough."\
--- Matt Mazewski, [*Commonweal*](https://www.commonwealmagazine.org/polanyi-ish)

"terrific book."\
--- Jamelle Bouie, [*New York Times*](https://twitter.com/jbouie/status/1450470921001750528)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="49%"}
### Reviews

[Commonweal](https://www.commonwealmagazine.org/polanyi-ish) ·
[Liberal Currents](https://www.liberalcurrents.com/freedom-and-the-common-good-mike-konczals-emfreedom-from-the-market-em/) ·
[Democracy Journal](https://democracyjournal.org/magazine/61/freedoms-just-another-word/) ·
[Financial Times](https://www.ft.com/content/239f31cb-57a3-43d3-ab3d-d18d068f4994) ·
[Foreign Affairs](https://www.foreignaffairs.com/reviews/review-essay/2021-02-16/market-value) ·
[Foreign Policy](https://foreignpolicy.com/2021/02/06/social-welfare-is-as-american-as-apple-pie/) ·
[Los Angeles Review of Books](https://lareviewofbooks.org/article/resisting-market-rule/) ·
[Wall Street Journal](https://www.wsj.com/articles/politics-of-markets-and-morals-11611961820) ·
[The Week](https://theweek.com/articles/963196/america-forgotten-how-free) ·
[Crooked Timber](https://crookedtimber.org/2021/01/26/freedom-from-the-market/) ·
[Karl Polanyi Project](https://karlpolanyiproject.blogspot.com/2021/01/polanyi-goes-to-america.html) ·
[National Review](https://www.nationalreview.com/2021/01/americas-supposedly-socialist-history/)
:::

::: {.column width="49%"}
### Media Appearances

[Majority Report](https://majorityreportradio.com/2021/01/27/1-27-americas-fight-to-liberate-itself-from-the-grip-of-the-market-w-mike-konczal) ([YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6i9XRNUNx8c)) ·
[Left Anchor](https://leftanchor.podbean.com/e/episode-175-americas-tradition-of-egalitarian-freedom-with-mike-konczal/) ·
[Vox's The Weeds](https://www.stitcher.com/show/voxs-the-weeds/episode/freedom-from-markets-81017167) ·
[Mass for Shut-Ins](https://massforshutins.libsyn.com/030-mike-konczal-freedom-from-the-market-2021) ·
[Lawyers, Guns and Money](https://www.lawyersgunsmoneyblog.com/2021/01/lgm-podcast-market) ·
[Dissent](https://www.dissentmagazine.org/online_articles/life-beyond-markets-mike-konczal)

### Excerpts and other Resources

[Medicare and desegregation at The Nation](https://www.thenation.com/article/politics/health-care-freedom-medicare/) ·
[Eight-hour workday at Boston Review](http://bostonreview.net/class-inequality-law-justice/mike-konczal-time-universal-measure-freedom)
:::

<!-- ==========================================================================
     NOW  —  the prose for each update lives in _now-entries.qmd (newest first).
     To post a new update, edit THAT file, not this one. The arrows below page
     back and forth through the entries; the "Updated <month>" label and the
     arrow states update automatically from each entry's data-date.
     ========================================================================== -->

## Now {#now}

A snapshot of what I'm working on, reading, and thinking about right now.

::::: {.mk-now role="group" aria-label="Browse Now updates"}

```{=html}
<button type="button" class="mk-now-arrow mk-now-arrow--left" data-now-newer aria-label="Show newer update">&#10094;</button>
```

:::: {.mk-now-viewport}

```{=html}
<a class="mk-now-meta mk-now-permalink" data-now-label href="now.html" aria-live="polite" title="Open this update on its own page">Updated</a>
```

{{< include _now-entries.qmd >}}

::::

```{=html}
<button type="button" class="mk-now-arrow mk-now-arrow--right" data-now-older aria-label="Show older update">&#10095;</button>
```

:::::

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  var root = document.getElementById("now");
  if (!root) return;
  var entries = Array.prototype.slice.call(root.querySelectorAll(".mk-now-entry"));
  if (!entries.length) return;
  // Newest first, by each entry's data-date ("YYYY-MM").
  entries.sort(function (a, b) {
    return (b.getAttribute("data-date") || "").localeCompare(a.getAttribute("data-date") || "");
  });
  var label = root.querySelector("[data-now-label]");
  var older = root.querySelector("[data-now-older]"); // right arrow: back in time
  var newer = root.querySelector("[data-now-newer]"); // left arrow: forward in time
  var months = ["January","February","March","April","May","June","July","August",
                "September","October","November","December"];
  var i = 0; // 0 = newest
  function fmt(d) {
    var p = (d || "").split("-");
    return "Updated " + (months[parseInt(p[1], 10) - 1] || "") + " " + (p[0] || "");
  }
  function render() {
    entries.forEach(function (el, idx) { el.hidden = idx !== i; });
    var d = entries[i].getAttribute("data-date");
    if (label) {
      label.textContent = fmt(d);                        // permalink to the standalone page
      label.setAttribute("href", "now.html#now-" + d);
    }
    if (older) older.disabled = i >= entries.length - 1; // nothing older
    if (newer) newer.disabled = i <= 0;                  // nothing newer
  }
  if (older) older.addEventListener("click", function () { if (i < entries.length - 1) { i++; render(); } });
  if (newer) newer.addEventListener("click", function () { if (i > 0) { i--; render(); } });
  render();
})();
</script>
```
